channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa Faces Severe Fuel Price Shock and Economic Strain
item.description: South Africa is experiencing an unprecedented fuel price surge, with petrol, diesel, and paraffin costs rising sharply. Petrol 93 increased by R5.50 per litre, Petrol 95 by R6.00, diesel by over R10 p...

item.title: France denies U.S. influence in G7 decision as Kenya replaces South Africa at summit
item.description: French officials have rejected claims from President Cyril Ramaphosa's office that South Africa was excluded from the upcoming G7 summit in Paris due to pressure from Washington. The French foreign mi...

item.title: Super Eagles set for friendly showdown with Iran in Turkey
item.description: The Super Eagles of Nigeria go face Iran in one international friendly match for Antalya, Turkey, as part of a four-nation invitational wey include Jordan and Costa Rica. This friendly match go help b...

item.title: Revolution | London and EXACT Technology Launch Integrated xBOLT Sensor for Real-Time Concrete Temperature Monitoring
item.description: Revolution | London has launched xBOLT™ for INSIGHT™, a new in-drum temperature monitoring system designed to provide real-time visibility of concrete temperature during transport. Developed in collab...

item.title: Pakistani worker killed in Abu Dhabi missile debris laid to rest in Bannu after repatriation
item.description: The body of Safir Ullah Eid Marjan, a Pakistani driver from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa who lost his life after being struck by missile debris in Abu Dhabi, has been returned to Islamabad for burial. The Pakis...

item.title: Three-year-old girl found safe after Seaview kidnapping incident in Karachi
item.description: Karachi Police have successfully recovered a three-year-old girl named Warisha who had gone missing from Seaview Beach on the third day of Eid ul Fitr. The incident occurred when the child was playing...

item.title: New Project Launched to Restore Ruvu Basin and Improve Water Security
item.description: Dar es Salaam and nearby areas are getting a major boost in water security through a new $1.94 million project focused on the Ruvu sub-basin. Known as the Nature-based Solutions for Catchment Restorat...

item.title: Raiders’ Ethan Strange ready for revenge against Sharks following last year’s semi-final loss
item.description: The Canberra Raiders are preparing to face the Cronulla Sharks this weekend, with a particular focus on making amends for last year’s elimination semi-final loss. Ethan Strange, who missed that critic...

item.title: Dar City starts BAL Kalahari Conference campaign against Johannesburg Giants in South Africa
item.description: Tanzania’s basketball champions, Dar City, are set to start their Basketball Africa League (BAL) Kalahari Conference journey today with a challenging game against Johannesburg Giants in Pretoria, Sout...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Newsom’s Dyslexia and Sparks Irony with 'Stupid Person' Remark
item.description: During a Cabinet meeting on March 26, 2026, former President Donald Trump triggered widespread backlash and mockery after declaring, “I don’t want a stupid person being president.” The comment came am...

item.title: Islamabad High Court schedules hearing on Imran Khan and Bushra Bibi’s sentence suspension pleas in Al-Qadir Trust case
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) has announced that it will hear petitions filed by former Prime Minister and PTI founder Imran Khan and his wife Bushra Bibi on March 31, seeking suspension of their res...

item.title: PMD forecasts light to moderate rain and thunderstorms in Karachi and Sindh districts on Saturday
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a weather outlook indicating chances of rain and thunderstorms in Karachi as well as several districts of Sindh province on Saturday, March 28, ...

item.title: Tanzanian Journalists Called to Improve Coverage on Climate Change Issues
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, media professionals have been urged to treat climate change as a constant area of reporting rather than waiting for disasters to strike. Speaking during a Media Stakeholder Reflectio...

item.title: The Spiritual and Scientific Dimensions of the Heart in Sufi Thought
item.description: This article by Syed Ali Imran explores the concept of 'qalb'—the heart—in the Islamic and particularly Sufi tradition of South Asia. It explains that Sufism, deeply rooted in the Quran, developed its...

item.title: Australia’s TGA considers changes to sunscreen labels and SPF ratings
item.description: Australia’s Therapeutic Goods Administration (TGA) has unveiled proposed reforms to improve sunscreen labelling and testing, aiming to restore consumer trust after recent reports revealed many product...

item.title: Government urges youth and creators to build wealth through talent and innovation
item.description: The government of Tanzania has launched a major initiative aimed at empowering young people, artists, and content creators to convert their creativity into sustainable income. According to the announc...

item.title: Tanzania and Zambia Collaborate to Improve Mineral Transport via TAZARA Railway Upgrade
item.description: Tanzania na Zambia wamekubaliana kuharakisha maboresho ya miundombinu ya usafirishaji wa madini ili kukuza sekta ya madini katika Afrika Kusini mwa bara. Hii ilitangazwa na Naibu Waziri wa Madini wa T...

item.title: Government announces detailed schedule for state funeral of Minister William Lukuvi
item.description: The Tanzanian government is organising a high-profile state funeral for the late William Lukuvi, who served as Minister of State in the Prime Minister’s Office responsible for Policy, Parliament, Coor...

item.title: US weighs risky ground assault on Iran’s Kharg Island to pressure Tehran
item.description: The United States is considering a ground operation to seize Iran’s vital Kharg Island, an oil export hub that handles about 90 per cent of the country’s crude shipments. President Donald Trump is rep...

item.title: Official portrait of former Manitoba Premier Brian Pallister unveiled at Legislature
item.description: Manitoba's Legislative Building recently unveiled the official portrait of Brian Pallister, who served as the province's Premier from 2016 to 2021. The ceremony marked a formal recognition of Palliste...
